discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current initial denial rate, and which three denial reason codes represent the largest volume?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Quantify denial problem magnitude and identify root cause categories (registration, authorization, coding, medical necessity)

discoveryQuestions.question: How many days does a typical prior authorization take from submission to final determination, and what's the clinical staff time burden?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Quantify PA friction cost in time, delay, and abandonment; identify payer-specific pain

discoveryQuestions.question: Walk me through your discharge planning process—from 'medically ready for discharge' to physical transfer. Where do delays typically occur?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Identify discharge barrier categories (SNF placement, DME, home care, physician orders, transportation, patient/family readiness)

discoveryQuestions.question: What's your current nurse vacancy rate, and how much are you spending annually on agency and travel nursing? How does that compare to three years ago?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Quantify workforce crisis magnitude and cost; establish trend line and urgency

discoveryQuestions.question: How do your HCAHPS scores compare to your top two local competitors, and which dimension is driving your biggest gap?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Identify competitive patient experience vulnerability and specific improvement opportunity

discoveryQuestions.question: What was your operating EBITDA margin last year, and what's the board's target for the next three years? What's the gap-closing plan?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Establish financial stress level, board expectations, and existing margin improvement initiatives

discoveryQuestions.question: How are you currently tracking 340B savings at the encounter level, and what was your last HRSA audit outcome?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Assess 340B program maturity, compliance risk, and revenue optimization gap

discoveryQuestions.question: What's your current Medicare Advantage Star rating, and which measures moved the most year-over-year? What's the improvement plan?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Identify Stars-specific vulnerability and existing quality improvement investments

discoveryQuestions.question: How do you currently identify and close HCC coding gaps for your MA population? What's your recapture rate and RAF score trend?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Assess risk adjustment program effectiveness and revenue capture opportunity

discoveryQuestions.question: Describe your current interoperability posture—are your FHIR APIs live for patient access? Have you received any information blocking complaints?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Assess Cures Act compliance status and information blocking exposure

discoveryQuestions.question: What's your current auto-adjudication rate, and how many FTEs are dedicated to manual claims processing and rework?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Quantify payer operational efficiency gap and labor cost of manual processing

discoveryQuestions.question: How many active clinical trials do you have, and how many are currently behind on enrollment? What's the typical screen failure rate?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Assess clinical development operational risk and trial execution capability

discoveryQuestions.question: What's your current HITRUST certification status, and are there any open critical control gaps? What's the remediation timeline?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Assess security framework maturity and contract eligibility risk

discoveryQuestions.question: How do you currently manage referral leakage? What percentage of your PCP referrals stay in-network, and what's the revenue impact of out-of-network leakage?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Quantify network integrity failure and revenue recovery opportunity

discoveryQuestions.question: What was your last security incident, and what's your current mean time to patch critical vulnerabilities? How does that compare to your cyber insurance requirements?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Assess cybersecurity posture, incident history, and insurance/contract compliance

discoveryQuestions.question: How are you managing behavioral health capacity? What's your average psychiatric ED boarding time, and how does that compare to your medical boarding time?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Identify behavioral health access crisis magnitude and operational impact

discoveryQuestions.question: What's your current supply chain inventory turn rate, and how much product do you write off annually due to expiration? What's your single-source dependency percentage?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Quantify supply chain inefficiency and resilience risk

discoveryQuestions.question: How many physician practices have you acquired in the last 24 months, and what percentage are at or above pre-acquisition productivity? What's driving the gap?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Assess acquisition integration effectiveness and physician alignment risk

discoveryQuestions.question: What's your current FWA detection rate, false positive rate, and recovery ratio? How does your SIU cost compare to recoveries?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Assess fraud program effectiveness and cost-inefficiency

discoveryQuestions.question: What are your top three strategic initiatives for the next 18 months, and how will technology enable or constrain them?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Map technology decisions to strategic priorities and identify constraint points

objections.objection: We already have an EHR/vendor that handles this
objections.underlyingConcern: Fear of duplication, integration complexity, or sunk cost rationalization with existing vendor
objections.reframeStrategy: Position as complementary layer that activates existing EHR data for specific outcome; demonstrate integration depth with their specific EHR; reference joint customers
supportingEvidence: joint customer references with same EHR
gap analysis showing EHR limitation in target area

objections.objection: We don't have budget this year / It's not in the capital plan
objections.underlyingConcern: Budget cycle rigidity, competing priorities, or lack of quantified ROI to justify reallocation
objections.reframeStrategy: Frame as self-funding through operational savings or penalty avoidance; offer phased implementation with first-phase ROI funding subsequent phases; propose operational expenditure vs. capital
supportingEvidence: operational budget vs. capital budget structuring

objections.objection: Our staff is already overwhelmed— we can't take on another implementation
objections.underlyingConcern: Change fatigue from prior implementations; fear of workflow disruption during staffing crisis; skepticism about vendor implementation support
objections.reframeStrategy: Emphasize managed implementation with dedicated resources; highlight automation that reduces rather than adds work; offer phased rollout with minimal frontline disruption
supportingEvidence: implementation staffing model with dedicated CSM
phased rollout plan with protected go-live dates

objections.objection: We need to see ROI proof from a similar organization first
objections.underlyingConcern: Risk aversion; skepticism of vendor-provided ROI; need for peer validation before commitment
objections.reframeStrategy: Offer reference calls with CFO/COO peers at comparable organizations; provide third-party validated case study with actual financials; propose paid pilot with documented ROI before full commitment
supportingEvidence: reference customer of similar size and EHR
pilot contract with ROI milestone gates

objections.objection: Security / Compliance team will never approve this
objections.underlyingConcern: Fear of compliance failure, audit finding, or breach from third-party solution; bureaucratic risk aversion
objections.reframeStrategy: Provide HITRUST/SOC 2 documentation up front; offer security architecture review with CISO; include BAA and security terms in initial contract draft; reference compliance-approved customers

objections.objection: We tried something like this before and it failed
objections.underlyingConcern: Post-traumatic implementation failure; fear of repeating same mistake; political risk of backing another failed initiative
objections.reframeStrategy: Acknowledge previous failure and diagnose root cause (technology, change management, vendor, timing); differentiate approach explicitly; offer risk-sharing or outcome-based contract

objections.objection: Our physicians won't adopt another tool / They're burned out on technology
objections.underlyingConcern: Physician resistance, EHR burnout, and skepticism that new tools add value rather than burden; fear of adoption failure
objections.reframeStrategy: Demonstrate workflow integration that eliminates clicks rather than adding them; show physician-designed interface; offer physician champion program; reference physician satisfaction scores from peer organizations
supportingEvidence: adoption rate data from peer organizations
time-motion study showing net time savings

objections.objection: The board won't support this level of investment
objections.underlyingConcern: Board risk aversion, capital allocation competition, or lack of board education on technology value; governance constraint
objections.reframeStrategy: Provide board-ready business case with industry benchmarks, peer adoption data, and risk-adjusted ROI; offer to present directly to board committee; structure as operational savings rather than new spend

objections.objection: Our data is too fragmented / dirty to support this
objections.underlyingConcern: Data quality anxiety; fear that underlying data issues will undermine solution value; organizational data governance immaturity
objections.reframeStrategy: Position solution as including data readiness assessment and cleansing; describe entity resolution, normalization, and validation as core capabilities; offer data quality guarantee or milestone-based payment

objections.objection: We need to finish [other initiative] first
objections.underlyingConcern: Sequential implementation preference; fear of concurrent change overload; legitimate dependency on other systems or processes
objections.reframeStrategy: Map how solution accelerates or de-risks the other initiative; identify integration points that make them mutually reinforcing; propose parallel track with shared resources; offer to engage after other initiative milestone

technologySystems.name: Electronic Health Record (EHR)
technologySystems.description: Core clinical documentation, order entry, medication management, and patient record system. Foundation for quality reporting, billing, and interoperability.

technologySystems.name: Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) Platform
technologySystems.description: End-to-end patient access, charge capture, claims management, payment posting, and collections system spanning front, mid, and back office.

technologySystems.name: Practice Management (PM) System
technologySystems.description: Ambulatory scheduling, registration, billing, and reporting system for physician practices and clinics.

technologySystems.name: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP)
technologySystems.description: Financial, supply chain, human resources, and procurement backbone for health systems and large provider organizations.

technologySystems.name: Human Capital Management (HCM) / Workforce Management
technologySystems.description: Staffing, scheduling, time/attendance, payroll, and float pool management for clinical and non-clinical workforce.

technologySystems.name: Enterprise Data Warehouse / Health Data Platform
technologySystems.description: Consolidated clinical, financial, and operational data repository enabling analytics, reporting, and population health insights.

technologySystems.name: Clinical Documentation Improvement (CDI) Platform
technologySystems.description: Concurrent and retrospective query management, coder workflow, and analytics for DRG accuracy and reimbursement optimization.

technologySystems.name: Computerized Physician Order Entry (CPOE) / Clinical Decision Support
technologySystems.description: Electronic ordering with embedded clinical decision support, drug-drug interaction checking, and protocol adherence.

technologySystems.name: Laboratory Information System (LIS)
technologySystems.description: Lab workflow, specimen tracking, result reporting, and instrument interface management for clinical and anatomic pathology.

technologySystems.name: Pharmacy Management System
technologySystems.description: Inpatient and outpatient pharmacy workflow, medication dispensing, inventory, 340B tracking, and clinical programs.

technologySystems.name: Picture Archiving and Communication System (PACS)
technologySystems.description: Medical image storage, retrieval, distribution, and display for radiology, cardiology, and enterprise imaging.

technologySystems.name: Customer Relationship Management (CRM) / Patient Engagement
technologySystems.description: Patient acquisition, retention, outreach, and engagement platform with campaign management and journey orchestration.

technologySystems.name: Health Information Exchange (HIE) / Interoperability Platform
technologySystems.description: Cross-organizational health data exchange, care summary distribution, query-based exchange, and FHIR API gateway.

technologySystems.name: Population Health Management Platform
technologySystems.description: Risk stratification, care gap identification, care management workflow, and quality reporting for value-based care.

technologySystems.name: Telehealth / Virtual Care Platform
technologySystems.description: Synchronous video visits, asynchronous messaging, remote patient monitoring, and virtual triage infrastructure.

technologySystems.name: Claims Management / Adjudication System
technologySystems.description: Claims intake, editing, adjudication, payment, and appeals management for health plans and TPAs.

technologySystems.name: Fraud Waste Abuse (FWA) Detection Platform
technologySystems.description: Pre-payment and post-payment claims analytics, provider profiling, and investigative case management for SIU.

technologySystems.name: Electronic Trial Master File (eTMF) / Clinical Operations Platform
technologySystems.description: Clinical trial document management, site monitoring, regulatory submission preparation, and quality management.

technologySystems.name: Cybersecurity / GRC Platform
technologySystems.description: Security operations, vulnerability management, compliance automation, risk quantification, and audit management.

technologySystems.name: Master Patient Index (MPI) / Enterprise Master Person Index (EMPI)
technologySystems.description: Patient identity matching, deduplication, and record linkage across enterprise systems and external exchange partners.

regulatoryFactors.name: HIPAA Privacy, Security, and Breach Notification Rules
regulatoryFactors.applicability: All covered entities (providers, payers, clearinghouses) and business associates
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Ongoing; breach notification within 60 days of discovery
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: $137–$2.068M per violation category annually; criminal penalties up to $250K + 10 years imprisonment for willful neglect

regulatoryFactors.name: CMS Hospital Inpatient Prospective Payment System (IPPS) and Quality Programs
regulatoryFactors.regulation: 42 CFR Part 412; CMS FY IPPS/LTCH PPS Final Rules
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Annual October 1 effective date for rate year
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: HRRP penalties up to 3% of base DRG payments; VBP ±2% payment adjustment; HACRP 1% penalty

regulatoryFactors.name: Medicare Advantage and Part D Star Ratings
regulatoryFactors.regulation: 42 CFR Part 422 Subpart D; CMS Star Ratings Technical Notes
regulatoryFactors.applicability: All Medicare Advantage and Part D plans
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Annual October release for following plan year
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Bonus payment loss of $50-150 PMPM; marketing restriction below 3.5 stars; contract termination below 2.0 stars for 3 consecutive years

regulatoryFactors.name: ONC Cures Act Final Rule – Information Blocking and Interoperability
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Health IT developers, HINs/HIEs, and healthcare providers
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Civil monetary penalties up to $1M per violation for IT developers; appropriate disincentives for providers

regulatoryFactors.name: FDA 21 CFR Part 11 – Electronic Records and Signatures
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Pharma, biotech, medical device, and clinical research organizations with FDA-regulated electronic records
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Ongoing; enforced during inspections and submissions
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Warning Letter, CRL, consent decree, product recall, import alert, debarment; no explicit fine structure but massive commercial impact

regulatoryFactors.name: EU MDR/IVDR – Medical Device and IVD Regulation
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Medical device and IVD manufacturers marketing in EU
regulatoryFactors.deadline: MDR fully applicable May 2021; IVDR fully applicable May 2022; transition periods extended
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Market withdrawal; Notified Body certificate revocation; national penalties vary (up to €500K-€5M in some member states)

regulatoryFactors.name: CMS Conditions of Participation / Conditions for Coverage
regulatoryFactors.applicability: All Medicare/Medicaid participating providers (hospitals, ASCs, SNFs, HHA, hospice)
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Termination of Medicare/Medicaid participation; civil monetary penalties; immediate jeopardy findings require 23-48 hour correction

regulatoryFactors.name: State Nurse Staffing Ratio Mandates
regulatoryFactors.regulation: Varies by state (CA, MA, OR, NY, CT, IL, WA, OH, etc.)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Acute care hospitals in states with enacted or proposed staffing ratio laws
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Varies; CA AB 394 effective 2004 (phased); other states 2024-2028 effective dates
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: State fines $10K-$100K+ per violation; potential licensure action; class action exposure

regulatoryFactors.name: 340B Drug Pricing Program Requirements
regulatoryFactors.regulation: Public Health Service Act Section 340B; HRSA 340B Program Guidance
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Covered entities (DSH hospitals, CHCs, FQHCs, Ryan White, black lung, hemophilia)
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Ongoing; HRSA audits and ADR process
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Removal from 340B program; repayment of duplicate discounts; OIG exclusion; manufacturer dispute risk

regulatoryFactors.name: Anti-Kickback Statute and Stark Law
regulatoryFactors.applicability: All entities and individuals furnishing items/services payable by federal healthcare programs
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Ongoing; OIG safe harbor and CMS Stark exception reliance
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Civil monetary penalties up to $100K per violation; False Claims Act treble damages; criminal penalties up to $25K + 5 years imprisonment; exclusion from federal programs

regulatoryFactors.name: HITRUST CSF Certification Requirements
regulatoryFactors.regulation: HITRUST Alliance CSF (not government regulation but contractual standard)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Organizations required by business partners, payers, or large health systems to demonstrate security maturity
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Loss of contract eligibility; exclusion from RFP shortlists; increased cyber insurance premiums

regulatoryFactors.name: No Surprises Act – Independent Dispute Resolution
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Health plans, providers, and facilities with out-of-network emergency and ancillary services
regulatoryFactors.deadline: January 1, 2022 effective; ongoing IDR process evolution
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Federal civil monetary penalties; state enforcement; contractual disputes; patient refund requirements

competitorFactors.name: Epic Systems Dominance in Large Health Systems
competitorFactors.description: Epic's ~35% US hospital market share and 90%+ of large health systems creates integration and interoperability ecosystem lock-in. Competitors must demonstrate Epic integration depth or target non-Epic segments.
competitorFactors.impactOnBuyingBehavior: Buyers prioritize Epic-certified solutions; non-integrated vendors face high barriers; interoperability via Care Everywhere is table stakes

competitorFactors.name: Optum (UnitedHealth) Vertical Integration
competitorFactors.description: Optum's ownership of Change Healthcare, largest US payer, and extensive provider assets creates competitive pricing pressure and data advantage. Bundled service+technology offerings difficult to compete against.
competitorFactors.impactOnBuyingBehavior: Buyers may fear Optum data leverage; independent vendors must differentiate on neutrality, customer service, or specialty depth

competitorFactors.name: Amazon / One Medical / PillPack Healthcare Entry
competitorFactors.description: Amazon's acquisition of One Medical and PillPack signals direct primary care, pharmacy, and virtual care competition. Threatens traditional patient acquisition and primary care networks.
competitorFactors.impactOnBuyingBehavior: Health systems accelerating digital front door, telehealth, and patient engagement investments; pricing transparency pressure

competitorFactors.name: CVS Health / Aetna Care Delivery Integration
competitorFactors.description: CVS's MinuteClinic, HealthHUB, and Aetna payer integration creates retail-based primary care and pharmacy benefit competition. Challenges traditional health system access models.
competitorFactors.impactOnBuyingBehavior: Health systems investing in urgent care, retail partnerships, and patient access to compete; pharmacy/payer vertical integration pressure

competitorFactors.name: Walmart Health Center Expansion and Contraction
competitorFactors.description: Walmart's health center expansion and subsequent closures create market uncertainty but validate retail health model. Remaining centers and partnerships still competitive threat in rural/suburban markets.
competitorFactors.impactOnBuyingBehavior: Rural health systems assessing competitive response; price transparency and cash-pay service pressure

competitorFactors.name: Teladoc / Livongo Virtual Care Consolidation
competitorFactors.description: Teladoc's Livongo acquisition creates integrated chronic care management and virtual visit platform. Competes with traditional care management and remote patient monitoring programs.
competitorFactors.impactOnBuyingBehavior: Employers and payers evaluating virtual-first models; health systems building or buying telehealth capabilities

competitorFactors.name: Veeva Systems Life Sciences Cloud Dominance
competitorFactors.description: Veeva's CRM, Vault, and clinical platform dominance in pharma/biotech creates high switching costs. Competitors must differentiate on specific modules or emerging segments (e.g., small biotech).
competitorFactors.impactOnBuyingBehavior: Pharma buyers default to Veeva ecosystem; niche vendors must prove integration or target non-Veeva workflows (e.g., manufacturing, real-world evidence)
sources: Life Sciences CRM Market Analysis IDC

competitorFactors.name: Medicaid MCO Market Consolidation (Centene, Molina, Elevance)
competitorFactors.description: Top 5 Medicaid MCOs control ~60% of managed Medicaid lives. State procurements favor incumbents with scale; new entrants face high capital and network adequacy barriers.
competitorFactors.impactOnBuyingBehavior: State Medicaid agencies seeking innovative niche players for supplemental services; incumbent MCOs buying enabling technology for competitive advantage

competitorFactors.name: Google / Microsoft / AWS Healthcare Cloud Wars
competitorFactors.description: Big Tech cloud and AI platforms (Google Health, Microsoft Health Data Services, AWS HealthLake) competing for healthcare data and AI workloads. Creates both threat and partnership opportunity.
competitorFactors.impactOnBuyingBehavior: Healthcare organizations evaluating cloud-native platforms; data governance and HIPAA/BAA concerns slow adoption; hybrid cloud most common

competitorFactors.name: Private Equity Hospital and Practice Consolidation
competitorFactors.description: PE-backed platforms (Steward, Prospect, TeamHealth, Envision) acquiring hospitals and physician practices at scale. Changes competitive dynamics and creates technology standardization demand.
competitorFactors.impactOnBuyingBehavior: PE platforms seek standardized, scalable technology stacks; preference for cloud-native, low-implementation solutions; cost optimization mandate

valueDomains.name: Patient Throughput
valueDomains.description: Volume of patients moved through care settings efficiently—admissions, transfers, discharges, and clinic visits per unit capacity

valueDomains.name: Length-of-Stay Reduction
valueDomains.description: Reducing inpatient days to geometric mean expectation while maintaining quality, freeing capacity and reducing cost

valueDomains.name: Readmission Reduction
valueDomains.description: Preventing unplanned 30-day readmissions through discharge planning, care transitions, and post-acute coordination

valueDomains.name: Denial Rate Reduction
valueDomains.description: Minimizing initial and final claim denials through front-end accuracy, clinical documentation, and payer relationship management

valueDomains.name: Claims Accuracy
valueDomains.description: Ensuring clean claim submission and auto-adjudication through coding accuracy, authorization compliance, and edits

valueDomains.name: Provider Productivity
valueDomains.description: Maximizing physician and advanced practice provider output in RVUs, visits, or wRVUs per FTE while maintaining quality

valueDomains.name: Nurse Productivity
valueDomains.description: Optimizing nursing hours per patient day, skill mix, and overtime while maintaining safety and satisfaction

valueDomains.name: Patient Satisfaction (HCAHPS)
valueDomains.description: Improving patient-reported experience across communication, responsiveness, and care transition dimensions

valueDomains.name: Care Gap Closure
valueDomains.description: Closing evidence-based preventive and chronic care gaps across attributed populations for HEDIS, Stars, and outcome improvement

valueDomains.name: Medication Adherence
valueDomains.description: Improving proportion of days covered for chronic medications to reduce downstream events and meet quality measures

valueDomains.name: Population Health Outcomes
valueDomains.description: Reducing preventable events, ED utilization, and total cost of care for attributed populations through proactive management

valueDomains.name: Compliance Risk Reduction
valueDomains.description: Minimizing regulatory, legal, and reputational exposure from HIPAA, CMS, FDA, and state program non-compliance

valueDomains.name: Operating Margin Improvement
valueDomains.description: Expanding the gap between revenue and operating expense through revenue cycle, cost, and throughput optimization
